foods.name: Yam
foods.description: A wholesome vegetable packed with essential vitamins, minerals, and dietary fiber. Important for digestive health and disease prevention.
foods.exerciseTimingBest: Any time — best with main meals. Avoid large servings of raw cruciferous pre-workout (gas risk)
foods.optimizationNote: Vegetables provide the fiber, minerals, and phytochemicals that no supplement can replicate. Aim for 5+ servings daily across all colors (each color provides different antioxidants). Cruciferous vegetables (broccoli, kale) are particularly protective against cancer.

foods.name: Yogurt (Plain, Low-Fat)
foods.description: A calcium-rich dairy product supporting bone health and providing protein. Important for growth and skeletal maintenance.
foods.exerciseTimingBest: Casein (yogurt, cheese) at night for sustained protein. Whey (milk) post-workout. Avoid pre-intense-cardio (nausea risk)
foods.optimizationNote: Dairy is the most efficient dietary calcium source (300mg per cup of milk). Calcium + vitamin D + weight-bearing exercise = the bone health triangle. Fermented dairy (yogurt, kefir) provides probiotics for gut health. Lactose-intolerant individuals can use fortified alternatives.
